data.description:
## Reproduction Steps

1. Configure a Jira project whose workflow contains a status with no entry in the reconciler's status map (e.g. a custom state like "In UAT").
2. Move a Jira issue that is bound to a **closed** local ticket into that unmapped status.
3. Run a reconcile pass with inbound sync enabled.

Expected: the unmapped status is skipped and surfaced as an alert (bridge_alerts + manifest tally); the local ticket's status is untouched.

Actual: inbound mapping defaults the unknown status to "open" (src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_differ.py:224-225), silently reopening the closed local ticket. This class of bug has already bitten once (ticket 929a). Audit Reliability finding #7 (MEDIUM).

## Why it matters

Reopening closed work corrupts board state, re-enters tickets into ready/next-batch dispatch, and invalidates completion attestations' meaning — all without any operator signal.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] An inbound Jira status with no mapping is skipped (no local status change), recorded as a deduped bridge alert, and tallied in the pass manifest (nonzero exit policy consistent with other per-mutation failures)
- [ ] Regression test: unmapped status inbound against a closed bound ticket — local ticket stays closed, alert emitted
- [ ] Existing mapped-status round-trips (including idea<->IDEA) unaffected; bridge suites green
- [ ] docs/jira-sync-setup.md documents the skip+alert behavior for unmapped statuses
data.title: Unmapped inbound Jira status defaults to open — can silently reopen closed local tickets
